Its investigation found no stolen assets, data, or deployed malicious code, and Consensys has since extended employee-grade vetting to outside staffing relationships and applied least-privilege access controls the FBI recommends against DPRK IT-worker infiltration.

title: New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
blurb: HollowGraph, a malware module tied to the Iranian-linked Cavern C2 framework, abuses the Microsoft Graph API to turn compromised Microsoft 365 mailbox calendars into a covert dead-drop, hiding encrypted commands and exfiltrated data in event attachments dated May 13, 2050. Group-IB counts at least 12 infected systems in espionage-focused attacks on Israeli organizations; defenders should audit Entra ID app registrations and Graph API activity, since a hardcoded client secret in logAzure.txt drives the authentication.

title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
blurb: Rapid7 recovered 1,048 files from an exposed malware delivery server, revealing an operator using generative AI to build, test, and document a phishing toolkit centered on CVE-2025-33053 (CVSS 8.8, now in CISA's KEV catalog), the WebDAV working-directory hijack. One campaign chain was already live against Windows users in Mexico, delivering an infostealer through a fake government ID-lookup site over WebDAV.

title: Infosec expert: Paidwork users' data pwned after 23M-record database dumped online
blurb: A 23M-record database belonging to Paidwork, the online gig-earning platform, was dumped on a cybercrime forum and confirmed by researcher Troy Hunt after affected users found their data in the leak. Exposed fields reportedly include email addresses and account details, so Paidwork users should treat their credentials as compromised, enable MFA, and rotate any passwords reused across other services.

title: Frontier LLMs couldn't help Hugging Face fight off evil agents
blurb: Hugging Face reported that frontier LLMs failed to reliably defend its infrastructure against autonomous attacker agents, missing or mishandling malicious activity that human analysts caught. The takeaway for defenders is that LLM-driven detection and triage still can't stand alone against agentic threats, so security teams deploying AI-assisted defense should keep human review in the loop rather than treating model output as authoritative.

title: OpenSSL Silently Fixes ‘HollowByte’ DoS Vulnerability
blurb: OpenSSL silently patched HollowByte, a pre-handshake DoS in which an 11-byte payload declares an oversized message body, forcing an unvalidated buffer pre-allocation of up to 131KB that glibc never returns to the OS, permanently bloating memory until the process is killed. Fixes shipped in 4.0.1 and were backported to 3.6.3, 3.5.7, 3.4.6, and 3.0.21, so operators running Apache, NGINX, Node.js, or any OpenSSL-linked service should upgrade immediately.

title: Chicago-based Empirical Security, which uses AI to help companies predict threats by monitoring exploited vulnerabilities, raised a $25M Series A (Chris Metinko/Axios)
blurb: Empirical Security, a Chicago-based startup that uses AI to predict threats by monitoring exploited vulnerabilities, raised a $25M Series A. The round backs a shift from CVSS-style static severity scores toward exploitation-based prioritization, aiming to help teams triage which vulnerabilities to patch based on real-world attacker activity rather than theoretical risk.

title: Malicious cloud customers can bring down the power grid
blurb: Researchers demonstrated that a malicious cloud customer can manipulate the power grid by rapidly cycling large compute workloads, creating synchronized load swings big enough to destabilize regional transmission systems. The attack requires no privileged access, only enough rented capacity in a data center dense region, and defenders have few levers beyond utility-side load shedding and provider ramp-rate limits on customer power draw.

title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
blurb: Fortinet is tracking "The TFF Trap," a phishing campaign active since late March that impersonates known companies via BEC lures to drop RATs and infostealers including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger on Windows systems. The loader is disguised as a TrueType Font (.ttf) file and executed by an AutoIT/Lua interpreter in memory, chaining fileless techniques and legitimate scripting environments to evade signature-based detection and static analysis.

title: Hugging Face uses open-weights Z.ai GLM 5.2 to battle attacker after commercial frontier model refusal
blurb: Hugging Face disclosed a breach in which an attacker used an autonomous AI agent to access a limited set of internal datasets and credentials for internal services before the company cut off access and hardened the environment. Commercial frontier models refused to analyze the attack logs because their guardrails can't distinguish exploit-building from defensive triage, so responders ran Z.ai's open-weight GLM 5.2 inside their own perimeter to keep attack data in-house.

title: Paidwork breach exposes sensitive data of 23 million user
blurb: A March 2026 breach at microtask platform Paidwork exposed data on 23,272,765 users, now indexed by Have I Been Pwned, including names, addresses, dates of birth, bank account numbers, transaction records, and bcrypt-hashed passwords. Paidwork has not acknowledged the intrusion; affected users should rotate any reused passwords, enable 2FA, and watch for phishing and financial fraud tied to the exposed banking data.

title: Remediating Vulnerabilities With LLMs: Inside Ivanti's Automation Push
blurb: Ivanti CSO Daniel Spicer detailed the vendor's internal program using frontier LLMs to both find and automatically remediate vulnerabilities, crediting the Claude 4.6 generation as the point the approach became viable. An LLM, not a researcher or Ivanti's own engineers, discovered CVE-2026-10520, the maximum-severity (CVSS 10) flaw in the Sentry mobile gateway disclosed last month. Spicer notes the effort is early-stage, with model cost and human-in-the-loop review still unresolved.

title: Italy fines WINDTRE €1.7 million over security flaws behind two data breaches
blurb: Italy's data protection authority, the Garante, fined WINDTRE €1.7 million after attackers social-engineered staff at two retail stores into granting system access, exfiltrating personal data on more than 365,000 customers and payment details for another 41,359. The regulator faulted unencrypted certificate and key storage plus internal APIs left out of vulnerability testing, where rate-limiting and CAPTCHA per the OWASP API Security Top 10 would likely have blocked the 2 million-request enumeration.

title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
blurb: Confirmed ransomware victims rose 389% year-over-year in 2025 to 7,831 globally, and nearly half of targeted companies paid up, per Sophos and Fortinet data attributing the surge to AI hacking tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT. The UK is advancing a ban on ransom payments by public sector bodies and critical national infrastructure, including the NHS, councils, and schools.

title: Health tech firm Craneware admits “significant volume” of customer and employee data exposed in cyber attack
blurb: Craneware, the Edinburgh-based provider of accounting and billing software to roughly 2,000 US hospitals, disclosed a contained security incident in which attackers exfiltrated a subset of customer, partner, and employee records along with a large volume of non-sensitive file names. The company has notified the UK ICO and FBI and appointed external forensics specialists, but has not detailed the volume of affected individuals or the intrusion vector.

title: Neo Security bags $100M to build the secure control layer for enterprise AI agents
blurb: Neo Security exited stealth with $100M led by Andreessen Horowitz and Bessemer Venture Partners to build a real-time control layer that inventories, attributes, and applies policy to enterprise AI agents. Founded by former SentinelOne, Wiz, and Palo Alto Networks staff, the startup targets the visibility gap SecOps teams face as Gartner projects agentic features in over 40% of enterprise applications by the end of 2026, up from 5%.

title: Neo Emerges From Stealth With $100M to Control and Secure Enterprise AI Software
blurb: Neo emerged from stealth with $100M in seed and Series A funding from Andreessen Horowitz, Bessemer, Craft Ventures, and Merlin Ventures for a control layer that governs AI agents, models, and MCP servers across enterprise environments. The platform maintains a continuous asset catalog, flags excessive privileges and misconfigurations, attributes each software action to a human, agent, or app identity, and enforces policies on tool calls and data movement.

title: ServiceNow pre-auth RCE exploited in the wild (CVE-2026-6875)
blurb: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-6875, a critical pre-authentication code-injection flaw in the ServiceNow AI Platform that lets unauthenticated attackers escape the script sandbox and fully compromise an instance and connected proxy servers. Self-hosted customers who have not applied the July 13 patch are exposed, though ServiceNow says its own hosted instances show no evidence of related activity.

title: SonicWall Zero-Days Exploited to Deliver Custom Malware for Weeks Before Patch
blurb: Two SonicWall SMA1000 zero-days, CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, were exploited by threat actor UTA0533 for roughly three weeks before SonicWall issued hotfixes on July 14, per Volexity, which found the group deploying custom KnuckleBall malware alongside a Java webshell and open-source proxy. CISA has added both flaws to its KEV catalog, bringing SonicWall's total to 17, and enterprises running the remote-access appliances should apply the hotfixes and hunt for the published IoCs.

title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
blurb: Group-IB detailed HollowGraph, an espionage implant that hijacks a compromised Microsoft 365 mailbox's calendar as a two-way command channel, planting tasking and exfiltrating encrypted files as attachments on events dated to 2050 so all traffic rides legitimate Microsoft Graph API calls. Because the activity mimics ordinary Microsoft 365 chatter and never contacts an attacker-owned server for payloads, network controls keyed to known-bad destinations have nothing to flag.

title: HOLLOWGRAPH malware turns Microsoft 365 calendars into an espionage channel
blurb: Group-IB researchers documented HOLLOWGRAPH, espionage malware that uses a compromised Microsoft 365 mailbox and the Graph API to hide command-and-control traffic inside calendar events dated to 2050, with DNS tunneling to refresh Entra ID credentials. The campaign hit 12 systems tied to Israeli entities and shows how trusted Microsoft services and legitimate Graph API access can carry data exfiltration past defenses watching for conventional C2 channels.

title: Microsoft 365 calendars become spy drop boxes in HOLLOWGRAPH campaign
blurb: Attackers in the HOLLOWGRAPH campaign are abusing Microsoft 365 calendar entries as covert dead drops, hiding stolen data and command-and-control instructions inside event fields that blend into normal Graph API traffic. The technique lets exfiltration ride legitimate Microsoft 365 channels that most enterprise security tooling trusts by default, forcing IT teams to treat Graph API activity and calendar writes as monitored surfaces rather than benign background noise.

title: ServiceNow’s sandbox escape RCE hole now exploited in the wild
blurb: A pre-auth sandbox-escape vulnerability in ServiceNow (CVE-2026-6875) that enables remote code execution is now under active exploitation in the wild, threat-intel firm Defused reported, though ServiceNow says it has seen no evidence affecting its hosted instances. Attackers are already varying their technique past the original proof of concept, so signature-based defenses may miss variants and self-hosted customers should apply ServiceNow's patch immediately.

title: Making highly available, multi-region Cloud Run services just got easier
blurb: Google Cloud added readiness probes and service health aggregation to Cloud Run, letting multi-region deployments detect regional failures and automatically fail traffic away to a healthy region within seconds. The capabilities remove the manual configuration and incident response that previously made resilient serverless deployments complex, and work with both single and multi-region services fronted by a global external application load balancer.

title: JadePuffer agentic attacks now target AI model data with ransomware
blurb: JadePuffer, the autonomous ransomware agent disclosed earlier this month, now deploys custom Go-based malware called EncForge that targets roughly 180 AI and ML file formats, including model checkpoints, SafeTensors weights, FAISS vector indexes, and training datasets, according to Sysdig. The agent adapted in real time during the intrusion, iterating through six Python delivery scripts in five minutes to encrypt assets that enterprise AI teams rarely back up like production data.

title: Sheetz replaces VMware at more than 830 stores
blurb: Sheetz is replacing VMware across more than 830 convenience-store locations with StorMagic's SvHCI hyperconverged stack, in one of the larger publicly disclosed VMware migrations to date. The chain has already converted over 600 stores remotely at roughly 200 per month, avoiding hardware upgrades and on-site visits by keeping its existing Dell PowerEdge servers, a signal of how Broadcom's VMware pricing is pushing enterprises toward alternatives.

title: New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
blurb: Group-IB researchers documented HollowGraph, a malware module that abuses the Microsoft Graph API and Microsoft 365 mailbox calendars as a covert command-and-control channel, hiding encrypted commands and exfiltrated data in calendar events dated to 2050. The tool is tied to the Iranian-linked Cavern framework and has infected at least 12 systems in an espionage campaign targeting Israeli organizations, using compromised Entra ID credentials that legitimate accounts make hard to distinguish from normal Graph traffic.

title: Microsoft will deploy AMD’s Helios rack-scale AI accelerator ‘at scale’ on Azure – Radeon Instinct MI455X and Epyc Venice power will be available through Redmond’s cloud infrastructure
blurb: Microsoft committed to deploying AMD's Helios rack-scale accelerators in volume across Azure and its own data centers, adding Instinct MI455X GPUs and Epyc Venice CPUs for frontier-model training and inference. The deal gives Azure and Microsoft Foundry customers an AMD-based alternative to Nvidia, though neither company disclosed the deployment's size in dollars or watts.

title: Microsoft will use AMD’s AI-optimized Helios racks in Azure
blurb: Microsoft will deploy AMD's Helios rack design in Azure, powering a new ND MI455X v7 instance family plus two other AMD-silicon-based series, with each rack packing 72 Instinct MI455 GPUs alongside Venice EPYC CPUs and Pensando DPUs. The move deepens Microsoft's multi-vendor accelerator strategy, giving Azure buyers an Nvidia alternative for inference workloads as AMD begins shipping the racks to customers later this year.

title: Cloudflare Internal DNS is now generally available
blurb: Cloudflare made Internal DNS generally available, extending its global network to provide authoritative and recursive DNS for private networks on the same control plane customers already use for public DNS and Zero Trust. Enterprise customers get it bundled with Cloudflare Gateway at no extra charge, consolidating split-horizon DNS onto one platform and putting pressure on legacy appliance vendors like Infoblox and cloud-native resolvers.

title: Malicious cloud customers can bring down the power grid
blurb: Researchers demonstrated that a malicious cloud tenant can manipulate a datacenter's power draw to trigger grid-level instability, exploiting the concentrated, synchronized load that hyperscale facilities place on regional utilities. The finding raises a shared-infrastructure risk for enterprises running workloads in colocation and public cloud, where a single abusive customer could cause outages that no individual tenant's own controls can prevent.

title: EU's AI labeling rules take effect next month
blurb: The EU's transparency rules requiring AI-generated content to be machine-readably labeled take effect next month, extending the AI Act's disclosure obligations to any provider serving EU users. Enterprise IT teams deploying generative systems now need labeling and watermarking baked into their pipelines before the deadline, adding a compliance workstream that touches every customer-facing model in the stack.

title: End-user AI spending to soar as CIOs grapple with costs
blurb: Global end-user spending on AI models and platforms will jump 63% year over year to $64B in 2026, according to a Gartner report, as CIOs tighten scrutiny of AI budgets around usage efficiency and measurable outcomes. Enterprise buyers are gravitating toward providers that prove value on cost, latency, performance, and reliability, and are pursuing contract protections like token-pricing floors, hard consumption limits, and multivendor routing to keep spend in check.

title: Windows Server Update Services buckle under Microsoft's metadata mountain
blurb: Microsoft's Windows Server Update Services is straining under the volume of update metadata it has to sync, with admins reporting failed synchronizations, bloated databases, and timeouts across on-prem WSUS deployments. The problem sharpens Microsoft's push toward cloud-based patching via Windows Autopatch and Intune, leaving shops that still rely on on-prem WSUS to either tune around the load or start planning a migration off the deprecated service.

title: OpenAI pushes new yardstick for measuring AI investments
blurb: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar floated a "useful-intelligence-per-dollar" metric for evaluating AI spend, as enterprises face mounting pressure to prove returns on their deployments. The framing conveniently reorients buyers away from raw token pricing, where OpenAI competes with cheaper rivals, toward outcome-based value that favors its higher-end models. IT leaders should treat vendor-supplied yardsticks skeptically and anchor ROI cases to their own workflow metrics rather than a supplier's preferred denominator.

title: UK's seventh prime minister in a decade says he'll ditch digital ID scheme
blurb: The UK's newly installed prime minister said he will scrap the government's national digital ID scheme, halting a program that had been positioned as mandatory infrastructure for accessing public services and right-to-work checks. The reversal strands the integrators and identity vendors that had begun bidding on the rollout and adds fresh delivery risk to any enterprise betting on UK government digital-identity contracts surviving a change of leadership.

title: OneDrive will block screenshots of sensitive documents, but only in Microsoft’s own browser.
blurb: Microsoft Edge will block screenshots of Purview-labelled PDFs opened in OneDrive and SharePoint starting in August, closing a gap where browser-viewed documents didn't enforce the copy restrictions already applied in desktop apps. The controls only work in Edge, so Microsoft is telling enterprises to steer employees onto its browser through Conditional Access, turning a data-protection feature into a lever for Edge adoption.

title: Microsoft expands Azure AI infrastructure with AMD's next-generation GPUs and CPUs
blurb: Microsoft expanded its Azure partnership with AMD to add the vendor's next-generation GPUs and CPUs alongside existing NVIDIA systems and its own Maia silicon. The multi-vendor approach gives enterprise buyers more compute options and pricing leverage while reducing Azure's dependence on NVIDIA supply, which has constrained AI capacity across every major cloud.

title: Making highly available, multi-region Cloud Run services just got easier
blurb: Google Cloud added readiness probes and service health aggregation to Cloud Run, giving multi-region services instance-level health checks that automatically fail traffic away from unhealthy regions within seconds via a global external load balancer. The capabilities cut the manual incident response that regional failover previously required, and service health works with both single- and multi-region deployments across public internet and VPC-internal traffic.

title: Cloudflare Internal DNS is now generally available
blurb: Cloudflare made Internal DNS generally available, providing authoritative and recursive private DNS on the same global network and control plane customers already use for public DNS and Zero Trust. It runs on the infrastructure behind 1.1.1.1 with no appliances to rack, folds split-horizon resolution into shared zones with per-view resolver policies, and comes at no additional charge for Enterprise customers on Cloudflare Gateway.

title: Branching databases like code: a CI/CD pattern for Lakebase, in production at Glaspoort
blurb: Glaspoort, a Dutch fiber operator, documented how it ships schema changes to Lakebase, the serverless Postgres OLTP that backs its project-management app, by branching a production-like database for every PR. Each pull request spins up an ephemeral, isolated copy so reviewers can test against fresh data without stepping on each other, with migrations held as the single source of truth. The trade-off is more short-lived databases to provision and reap, absorbed by treating them as disposable IaC alongside the app's CI/CD.

title: Automate work item assignment with a "Work item created" trigger
blurb: GitLab added a "Work item created" trigger to its Duo Agent Platform, firing flows automatically the moment an issue is created instead of requiring a manual UI action like a mention or assignment. The event-driven trigger lets triage and routing flows weigh team capacity and availability to assign work in seconds, removing the human handoff that previously gated every Duo Flow.

title: Sheetz replaces VMware at more than 830 stores
blurb: Sheetz is replacing VMware across more than 830 convenience stores with StorMagic's SvHCI hyperconverged stack, one of the larger publicly announced VMware exits since Broadcom's acquisition reshaped licensing. The migration runs entirely remotely on the chain's existing Dell PowerEdge R440 and R450 servers, with 600-plus stores already cut over at roughly 200 conversions per month and no hardware refresh or on-site visits required.

title: One login to rule them all: centralized auth for internal tools with Caddy
blurb: The author walks through putting internal tools behind a single Caddy reverse proxy with the caddy-security (AuthCrunch) plugin, authenticating against the company's existing Azure AD tenant over OAuth2/OIDC so each new tool needs only two lines of config and no separate user database. Caddy's automatic Let's Encrypt handling removes certbot cron jobs and expired-cert incidents, and the post covers the two rough edges that needed extra attention to get the OIDC flow working.

title: Malicious cloud customers can bring down the power grid
blurb: Researchers at Zhejiang University showed that malicious cloud tenants can weaponize normal GPU training workloads, timing power swings at synchronization points like checkpointing to push oscillations past 6,000 Hz and match critical grid frequencies. Their Bit2Watt attack drove 46.8% total harmonic distortion and a negative damping ratio on a simulated 1-MW grid, and stays covert because it runs entirely inside authorized workload paths that cloud-provider monitoring is unlikely to flag.

title: Why Your Top Keywords Are Quietly Losing Volume
blurb: Brainlabs analyzed 1.35 million UK keywords across nine categories and found head terms in structural decline in seven of them, while longtail queries grow and AI Overviews concentrate exactly where the volume is shifting. Brands that lean on high-volume head terms for organic traffic should reallocate toward longtail and conversational queries now, since AI Overviews are also surfacing on commercial searches, not just informational ones.

title: Google’s AI cited Facebook 19.5 million times, new research finds
blurb: BrightEdge found Facebook appeared as a source in 19.5 million Google AI Overviews, versus 877,000 for Instagram and 78,000 for TikTok, meaning roughly one in 15 searches now pulls from social media. Google's AI appears to weight platforms by job, leaning on Facebook for local and timely queries and Instagram for lifestyle and shopping, so visibility in AI answers now depends on owned posts and threads rather than website rankings.

title: Before you refresh that page, run this test
blurb: Content decay now has four distinct causes, and treating every organic traffic drop as a stale-content problem no longer works. In 2026, fewer than one in three Google searches sends a click to the open web, roughly 68% end without a click, and pages with an AI Overview lose about 58% of top-organic clicks even when rankings and demand hold. Diagnose the fingerprint first: ranking decay, AI click theft, shifting intent, or declining demand each leave different signals in the data you already have and require a different fix.

title: Branded search is becoming a less reliable proxy for brand demand
blurb: Branded search demand fell 11.1% over the past month in one agency's client portfolio despite a stable auction environment, but the drop reflects changed behavior rather than weaker demand. AI Overviews now appear in 95.9% of commercial searches for a core client query, up from 57.2% under a year ago, so AI answers resolve comparison and consideration before users ever type a branded query. Since branded acquisition still runs 76.6% cheaper than non-branded, teams relying on branded search volume as a demand signal risk under-reading pipeline and misallocating budget.

title: How to run a CTV incrementality test (and why most fail)
blurb: Most CTV incrementality tests fail at the design stage, not the media stage, producing an uplift number that looks convincing but proves nothing. The usual mistake is comparing people who saw the ad against those who didn't, which measures targeting rather than the ad's actual impact, since exposed viewers already stream more and skew toward interest. Building a randomized holdout control group before launch is what turns the result into a defensible number, and the pressure is rising: cross-platform measurement was a top priority for 72% of advertisers heading into 2026, up from 64%.

title: Consensys Halted MetaMask Releases Over North Korea-Linked Contractor
blurb: Consensys suspended MetaMask product releases earlier this year after finding that a contractor with North Korea links had access to the wallet's code for about a month, including sections handling third-party fiat payment providers. The company's investigation found no stolen assets, data, or malicious code, and traced the hire to a third-party staffing provider rather than its direct channel.

title: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and what it means for users
blurb: Cardano activated its Van Rossem hard fork on July 18, moving the mainnet to protocol version 11, lowering smart contract execution costs and laying groundwork for the planned Ouroboros Leios scaling upgrade. It is the first upgrade in Cardano's history proposed, debated, and ratified entirely through the network's onchain governance system rather than by founding engineering firm Input Output.

title: Lighter Makes Stock Tokens Eligible Collateral on Robinhood Chain
blurb: Lighter, the zero-knowledge perpetuals exchange, now accepts Robinhood Stock Tokens as trading collateral on Robinhood Chain, letting users post tokenized equities like NVDA, GOOG and AAPL as margin for perpetual futures. The move expands collateral beyond the USDG stablecoin that had served since the chain's July 1 mainnet launch, though the tokens remain off-limits to Robinhood's roughly 24 million US-funded accounts under securities restrictions.

title: Allbridge Halts Core Bridge After $1.65M Flash Loan Exploit
blurb: Allbridge paused its Core bridge on July 20 after an attacker used a $1.12M flash loan from Kamino to distort its Solana stablecoin pools and drain roughly $1.65M, per PeckShield and CertiK. The exploit hit a side-by-side USDC/USDT pool, the multi-stablecoin setup Allbridge had pledged to eliminate after a nearly identical $650K flash loan attack on its BNB Chain pools in 2023.

title: Hyperliquid to Open HIP-4 Prediction Markets Behind 500,000 HYPE Stake
blurb: Hyperliquid said a future HIP-4 enhancement will let anyone deploy permissionless prediction markets by staking 500,000 HYPE, roughly $30M, which validators can slash for markets that are poorly defined or settled incorrectly. Deployers earn up to 50% of trading fees and settle every outstanding market before withdrawing, pushing Hyperliquid deeper into a sector dominated by Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: Trump’s crypto ethics stance leaves Clarity Act negotiations in limbo
blurb: Trump declined to sign off on ethics language during a July 16 meeting with Sens. Bernie Moreno and Cynthia Lummis, leaving the Clarity Act's final sticking point unresolved as negotiators seek limits on how officials profit from digital assets. The provisions target Trump's memecoins and World Liberty Financial, which recent disclosures tied to hundreds of millions in family income, complicating the bill's path to a federal crypto market-structure framework.

title: AI compute stocks bounce as Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts
blurb: Hut 8 and IREN shares jumped as much as 17% and 19% Monday after signing multibillion-dollar AI infrastructure deals, with Hut 8 landing a 15-year, $9.8B lease for its Beacon Point campus in Texas and IREN adding $2.8B in multiyear cloud contracts. The rally lifted other bitcoin miners repositioning as AI compute providers, easing recent concerns that demand for data center capacity was cooling.

title: Vietnam sets new crypto trading fines on par with drunk-driving penalties
blurb: Vietnam introduced fines of roughly $1,140 to $1,900 for domestic investors who trade crypto outside a licensed service provider, putting the penalties on par with the country's drunk-driving offenses. The rules push retail activity toward state-sanctioned exchanges as Vietnam builds out a formal licensing regime for one of Asia's highest crypto-adoption markets.

title: Hut 8 fully commercializes 1 GW Texas AI campus with second $9.8B lease as IREN signs $2.8B in contracts; shares climb double digits
blurb: Hut 8 secured a second $9.8B lease to fully commercialize its 1 GW Texas AI data center campus, while rival miner IREN signed $2.8B in new AI compute contracts. Both stocks climbed double digits as the bitcoin-mining-to-AI-infrastructure pivot converts idle hashrate capacity into long-term data center revenue.

title: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
blurb: Brazil's securities regulator, the CVM, formed a 14-department working group to draft an experimental framework for tokenized securities, with a first proposal due within 60 days of installation. The rules will govern registration, custody, trading, and settlement on distributed ledgers for a domestic real-world asset market that has reached roughly 12 billion reais ($2.34 billion).

title: Former NY Governor Cuomo joins OKX board, touts 24/7 tokenized trading
blurb: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo joined OKX's board, arguing the exchange's round-the-clock tokenized trading could pull more international capital into US markets. The appointment adds political weight to OKX's US expansion, roughly a year after the exchange settled with the DOJ for $505M over unlicensed money-transmitting charges and relaunched its American operations.

title: Bitmine expands Ethereum treasury to 5.78 million ETH, repurchases 5.5 million shares
blurb: Bitmine Immersion Technologies expanded its Ethereum treasury to 5,777,468 ETH, worth about $10.8 billion and equal to 4.8% of supply, keeping it the largest corporate ether holder behind only Strategy in total digital asset treasury size. The company also repurchased 5.5 million shares under its $4 billion buyback, with 85% of its ETH staked and generating roughly $247 million in projected annualized yield.

title: Exodus to cut 25% of global workforce in payments shift
blurb: Exodus Movement (EXOD) is cutting about 25% of its global workforce as it pivots from crypto wallets toward stablecoin payments and card infrastructure, building a full-stack payments platform on its Monavate and Baanx acquisitions. The company expects $10M to $13M in annual operating expense savings by 2027 against $2.5M to $3.5M in restructuring charges, with EXOD still down nearly 85% year-over-year.

title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slowed ether purchases as it bought back $86 million in stock
blurb: Bitmine slowed its Ethereum accumulation to 7,430 ETH last week, worth about $14 million, redirecting capital to repurchase 5.5 million shares at an average $15.62 under its $4 billion buyback program. The treasury firm now holds 5.78 million ETH, roughly 4.8% of circulating supply, and stakes 4.92 million of it for a projected $247 million in annualized revenue.

title: Bernstein lifts Robinhood target to $160, sees prediction markets revenue overtaking crypto
blurb: Bernstein raised its Robinhood price target to $160 from $137, projecting that prediction markets revenue will overtake crypto trading as a growth driver alongside the Rothera acquisition and the planned Robinhood Chain. The note reframes Robinhood less as a crypto-exposure play and more as a diversified trading platform, with prediction markets positioned as the faster-scaling segment.

title: Benchmark backs Exodus after 25% workforce cut, says stablecoin pivot is being overlooked
blurb: Benchmark reaffirmed its backing of Exodus after the self-custody wallet maker cut roughly 25% of its workforce, arguing the market is overlooking the company's pivot toward stablecoin infrastructure. Exodus said late Friday it expects the restructuring to generate $10M to $13M in annualized cost savings.

title: AI-assisted hacking is outpacing traditional code reviews, NEAR co-founder says
blurb: NEAR co-founder Illia Polosukhin argued that AI-assisted hacking, which he traces to the October Balancer exploit, is now finding vulnerabilities faster than human code review can patch them. He called for a shift to formal verification, pointing to Zcash's Project Tachyon, where AI-assisted proof generation cut work that once took years down to weeks.

title: Strategy sells $263.5 million in MSTR shares, buys no bitcoin as USD reserve tops $3.2 billion
blurb: Strategy sold $263.5M in MSTR shares last week and made no bitcoin purchases, growing its USD reserve past $3.2 billion. The company still holds roughly 4% of bitcoin's 21 million supply cap, worth about $54.7 billion, but the pause signals a shift toward building dollar liquidity over stacking more BTC.

title: Bitmine Slowed ETH Buys to Smallest Weekly Pace Since June 2025
blurb: Bitmine Immersion Technologies bought 7,430 ETH over the past week, its smallest weekly purchase since launching its treasury strategy on June 30, 2025, as it redirected capital into a $4B share buyback that repurchased 5.5 million shares. The company now holds 5,777,468 ETH worth roughly $10.9B, or 4.8% of supply, keeping it the largest corporate ether holder as it nears its 5% target.

title: HYPE Spot ETFs Log First Weekly Outflow Since May
blurb: Spot Hyperliquid (HYPE) ETPs logged their first weekly outflow since launching in May, shedding $7.26 million in the week ending July 17 and ending a nine-week inflow streak. Net assets under management fell 12.7% to $306.03 million as HYPE dropped over 8%, even as Bitcoin, Ether, XRP, and Solana funds together pulled in more than $188 million.

title: National Bank of Canada Weighs SRT Tied to Project Finance Deals
blurb: National Bank of Canada is gauging investor demand for a significant risk transfer deal tied to its project-finance loans, joining a widening group of banks using SRTs to free up balance-sheet capacity. The trend reflects how lenders are increasingly offloading credit risk to private-capital buyers to meet regulatory capital requirements without shrinking their loan books.

title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Data Center
blurb: BlackRock is looking to sell more than $12B of bonds to finance a Meta data center campus in El Paso, Texas, the latest in a run of debt deals bankrolling tech's AI buildout. The transaction shows how private-credit and capital-markets desks are absorbing the financing load for hyperscaler infrastructure, moving AI capex off Big Tech balance sheets and into structured debt held by institutional investors.

title: Trump’s crypto ethics stance leaves Clarity Act negotiations in limbo
blurb: Trump declined to endorse ethics provisions during a July 16 meeting with Sens. Bernie Moreno and Cynthia Lummis, leaving the Clarity Act's path to passage unresolved as negotiators wait on language limiting how officials profit from digital assets. The sticking point is Trump's own crypto wealth, including his memecoins and World Liberty Financial, which recent disclosures tied to hundreds of millions of dollars.

title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
blurb: Natural raised a $30M Series A led by Forerunner to build payment infrastructure for autonomous AI agents, positioning it against Stripe. The startup acts as an orchestration layer that lets agents pay vendors, collect funds, and transact with humans and other agents without human authorization, bypassing the human-in-the-loop bottleneck in credit-card and ACH rails.

title: Natural, which aims to help companies let their AI agents make autonomous payments, raised a $30M Series A led by Forerunner, bringing its total raised to $40M (Marina Temkin/TechCrunch)
blurb: Natural raised a $30M Series A led by Forerunner to help companies give their AI agents the ability to make autonomous payments, bringing its total funding to $40M. The round reflects growing demand for agent-native payment infrastructure that can handle authorization, spending limits, and settlement without a human in the loop, a gap that existing card rails and KYC flows were not built to fill.

title: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
blurb: Brazil's securities regulator, the CVM, formed a 14-department working group to draft an experimental framework for tokenized securities, with a first proposal due to its board within 60 days and a broader review running 120 days. The rules will cover registration, custody, trading, and settlement on distributed ledgers, addressing ownership records, private-key custody, and liability as Brazil's roughly $2.34B real-world asset market keeps growing.

title: Bernstein lifts Robinhood target to $160, sees prediction markets revenue overtaking crypto
blurb: Bernstein raised its Robinhood price target to $160, projecting that prediction markets revenue will overtake crypto trading as a top growth driver alongside its Rothera and Robinhood Chain initiatives. The call signals Wall Street increasingly views Robinhood's event-contract and on-chain expansion, not equities or crypto, as the swing factor for its revenue mix.
